author: @WileHeCoyote
text: Very cool mate! Is there a practical circuit length "maximum" with the ink and the L.E.D.'s or can you draw what ever design you need with adequate conductivity?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
Thanks mate! 
You know what, i haven't tried to reach the "maximum" circuit length.
But I can tell you that i have made some pretty big/long circuits. It should defiantly keep adequate conductivity all the way through. ( I would put 2 coats of ink though, just to be sure, if you're working ink big circuits)
Hope this helps!

replies.author: @globalmedia2730
replies.text:
You are talking about resistance and voltage drop. To calculate the resistance of a wire, you need to know three things:
  -  its length  
  -  its cross-sectional area 
  - The resistivity of the material   
       Resistance = resistivity × length / area
Then to calculate the resistivity. You can rearrange it to get a formula as below:
       resistivity = resistance × area / length 
Then Just calculate the voltage drop of the conductor.

author: @anthonydonnelly4069
text:
Dead cool man.   I've been looking for a picture frame to suite my needs
but not found one with the aesthetics and required depth
but I'm sure one will turn up.
It's intended to be just a picture during the day but light up at dusk 
as background hallway lighting/picture.
I'm observing your techniques for reference.
I'm merely a beginner amature in electronics so always watching out for inspiration to backup my
highly limited electronic abilities. 
The arduino has come to light as a possible control source thanks to yours and Roberts work with these devices.
Always been put off because of the time element learning the Arduino electronics/programming.
Nevertheless I will persist until I get what I want.  Just doing it for fun really.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text:
Hi Anthony, Thanks mate!
This one is a really cool idea!
I was only a beginner a few weeks ago (still am lol), But its all about giving it a go.
The arduino is an awesome piece of equipment and only cost a few quid, its kinda easy getting the hang of the basics, and there is defiantly sketches and shields out there that would make this project easier.
Give it a go mate, because that would be very interesting!
